_help._note: Allowed values + requirements per field (this _help block is ignored by the gate — keep or delete). Run `plumb schema` for the full reference.
_help.task_id: required — ticket/issue/branch id (also the receipt filename)
_help.agent_id: required — which agent or human did the work
_help.intent: required — what + why, plain language — the semantic review reads this
_help.self_modifying: one of: true | false — required — MUST be true if changed_files touch policy.protected_paths; touching one with false is a hard fail; true routes to human review
_help.validation_plan: required — each: { command, reason, required, id?, ci_covered? }
_help.validation_plan[].required: one of: true | false — required — is this check mandatory
_help.validation_plan[].id: optional — optional step id; evidence is matched to it via execution_evidence[].step (robust to a command wording diff)
_help.validation_plan[].ci_covered: one of: true | false — optional — step is corroborated by the ci-evidence gate (real CI run), not manual evidence — may be 'skipped'; also auto-recognized when command matches a policy ci_evidence_checks entry
_help.execution_evidence: required — each: { command, status, output_ref?, skip_reason?, step? }
_help.execution_evidence[].status: one of: passed | failed | skipped — required — required steps must be 'passed' (unless CI-covered); use skip_reason when 'skipped'
_help.execution_evidence[].step: optional — optional id of the validation_plan step this evidence is for (matches validation_plan[].id)
_help.changed_files: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — don't hand-edit
_help.base_sha: optional — pinned merge-base the diff was computed against — makes gate verification deterministic; set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit
_help.diff_sha256: required — set by `plumb receipt --write` — never hand-edit (computed from base_sha)
_help.result_summary: required — what changed + how it was verified

intent: Plumbline v0.7.0 — the proof-carrying code-lifecycle + gate-precision release. Adds `plumb receipt generate` (honest auto-synthesized receipt for MCP-authored changes: validation deferred to repo CI via ci_covered, self_modifying auto-detected from protected_paths), a per-repo `lifecycle: review|auto_merge` knob that enables GitHub-native auto-merge on a terminal PASS, a conservative documented default for protected_paths (high-consequence surfaces only, not broad code trees) so REVIEW stays meaningful and non-protected green PRs can flow, and a follow-up flood fix (confidence threshold + one consolidated issue per PR + auto-close on merge).

validation_plan.reason: Full node:test suite — proves the four features: receipt-generate shape validity + self_modifying detection + ci_covered validation, lifecycle auto_merge enabling GH auto-merge only on PASS, protected-paths default + override, and follow-up threshold/consolidation/auto-close.
tsc --noEmit-equivalent full compile — proves the TypeScript typechecks across the new modules and the edited cli/github/types.
Rebuild dist/index.js so the committed prebuilt bundle the Action runs is up to date (dist-check clean).

execution_evidence.output_ref: # tests 318 # pass 318 # fail 0 (includes the new receipt-generate, protected-paths-default, auto-merge, and followup-consolidation suites)
tsc exit 0 — clean compile, no type errors
esbuild → dist/index.js 292.5kb, Done — dist rebuilt and committed

result_summary: Shipped four changes in src/receipt-generate.ts (new), src/cli.ts (receipt generate + lifecycle auto_merge wiring + followups close verb), src/github.ts (enableAutoMerge via enablePullRequestAutoMerge, consolidated follow-ups + auto-close), src/types.ts (DEFAULT_PROTECTED_PATHS + lifecycle + follow_ups policy fields), and templates/policy.json + README + CHANGELOG. Verified: full test suite 318/318 pass (18 new tests across 4 suites), clean tsc build, dist/index.js rebuilt (dist-check clean), and an end-to-end smoke test of `plumb receipt generate` in a scratch repo (non-protected → self_modifying false + shape PASS; protected workflow file → self_modifying true; byte-identical re-run confirms idempotency). Version bumped 0.6.2 → 0.7.0. self_modifying is true because the change edits protected surfaces (src/github.ts, src/types.ts) — routes to human REVIEW, as designed.
